creator: mirko.steiner@slashdevslashnull.de
text:
when i boot up my computer the devd starts the moused for the usb moused device he founds.

when moused gets started that way, xorg doesnt find the mouse, when i kill the running moused from devd, and start moused via /etc/rc.d/moused it works.

the ONLY differece between starting moused from devd and starting moused directly is, that via devd the moused writes the PID into another file e.g.:

/var/run/moused.ums0.pid

when starting with /etc/rc.d/moused the PID is stored in a file named:

/var/run/moused.pid

in the later case, xorg finds the mouse... by the way: i dont use a configfile for xorg.

i dont know if the bug is on xorg or on BSD :(

How-To-Repeat: see full description
